Done! I analyzed both documents and saved the analysis to `secrets/docs/incident-changelog-analysis.txt`.

## Key Findings:

**The changelog releases are NOT directly related to the incident.**

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Incident** | Database breach via compromised service account (`svc-analytics`) |
| **Root Cause** | Credentials exposed in public GitHub repo (separate data-pipeline system) |
| **Changelog** | Covers webapp REST API endpoints (GET/POST /api/users, health checks) |

The incident was an **infrastructure security failure** (credential exposure), not a webapp code issue. The changelog describes API features while the breach involved direct database access through a service account. The last webapp release (v0.3.0) also predates the incident by ~8 months.
